description: Implement the 'open tab' feature on plugin cards in the side panel. Clicking a plugin's icon either focuses an existing matching tab (cycling through multiple tabs on repeated clicks), or opens the plugin's homepage URL in a new tab when no matching tab exists. This requires: (1) plumbing tab info to the side panel, (2) a background message handler for tab focus/open, (3) PluginIcon becoming an interactive click target, (4) Storybook story updates, and (5) E2E tests.

userStories.title: Add tab info to side panel state and bridge
userStories.description: Plumb per-plugin tab information (tab count, tab IDs) from the background script to the side panel so the PluginCard knows how many matching tabs exist and can request tab focus. Add a PluginTabSummary type to ConfigStatePlugin with tabCount and tabIds.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigStatePlugin in shared/src/index.ts has optional tabs?: { tabId: number; url: string; title: string; ready: boolean }[] field
background-message-handlers.ts handleBgGetFullState includes tabs array from the tab state cache for each plugin
Side panel PluginState (via bridge.ts) includes tabs data from ConfigStatePlugin
When a plugin has 3 matching tabs, the side panel receives tabs array with 3 entries
When a plugin has 0 matching tabs (closed state), tabs is undefined or empty array
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/shared/src/index.ts (add tabs field to ConfigStatePlugin)
- platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts (include tabs in getFullState response)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts (PluginState inherits tabs from ConfigStatePlugin)

Context:
ConfigStatePlugin (shared/src/index.ts:307-330) already has `tabState: TabState` (the aggregate state). We need to add the individual tab list so the side panel knows how many tabs exist and their IDs.

The background script already has tab info. In background-message-handlers.ts handleBgGetFullState (line ~142-251), the tab state is read from a session storage cache. The cache stores `{ state: TabState; tabs: PluginTabInfo[] }` per plugin (set by tab-state.ts). The code currently only reads `tabInfo?.state` and discards the `tabs` array.

The PluginTabInfo type already exists in shared/src/index.ts:275-284:
```typescript
export interface PluginTabInfo {
  tabId: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  ready: boolean;
}
```

Changes:
1. In shared/src/index.ts ConfigStatePlugin, add after `tabState: TabState;`:
```typescript
/** Individual matching tabs with per-tab details. Only present when tabState is not 'closed'. */
tabs?: PluginTabInfo[];
```

2. In background-message-handlers.ts handleBgGetFullState, where the code reads tab state from cache (look for `tabInfo?.state ?? 'closed'`), also include the tabs array:
```typescript
tabState: tabInfo?.state ?? 'closed',
tabs: tabInfo?.tabs,
```

3. In bridge.ts, PluginState is `Omit<ConfigStatePlugin, 'tools'> & { tools?: WireToolDef[] }` — the tabs field will flow through automatically since it's inherited from ConfigStatePlugin.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change how tab state is computed or cached — only read the existing tabs array and pass it through.
- Do NOT change the PluginTabInfo type.
- The tabs field MUST be optional (undefined when tabState is 'closed' for efficiency).
- The handleBgGetFullState function also builds state from server cache (serverPlugin spread) and PluginMeta spread — make sure tabs comes from the tab state cache, not from those sources.

userStories.title: Add bg:openPluginTab message handler for tab focus and open
userStories.description: Add a new background message handler bg:openPluginTab that either focuses an existing matching tab or opens the plugin's homepage URL in a new tab. Supports cycling through multiple tabs on repeated calls.
acceptanceCriteria: bg:openPluginTab message type is handled in the background script dispatch map
When called with a plugin name that has matching tabs, it activates (focuses) one of them using chrome.tabs.update(tabId, { active: true }) and chrome.windows.update(windowId, { focused: true })
When called multiple times for the same plugin with multiple tabs, it cycles through tabs in round-robin order (not always focusing the same tab)
When called for a plugin with no matching tabs and the plugin has a homepage, it opens a new tab with chrome.tabs.create({ url: homepage })
When called for a plugin with no matching tabs and no homepage, it returns an error or does nothing gracefully
The handler reads plugin metadata from chrome.storage.local to get urlPatterns, excludePatterns, and homepage
The handler uses findAllMatchingTabs (from tab-matching.ts) to find existing tabs, respecting excludePatterns

userStories.title: Make PluginIcon clickable to open/focus matching tabs
userStories.description: Separate the PluginIcon from the accordion trigger in PluginCard and make it an independent click target that calls openPluginTab. Add visual feedback (cursor, hover effect) and a tooltip describing the action.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginIcon is no longer part of the AccordionPrimitive.Trigger — it is rendered before it as an independent clickable element
Clicking the icon calls openPluginTab(plugin.name) via the bridge
When tabState is 'ready' with 1 tab, tooltip says 'Open {displayName}'
When tabState is 'ready' with N>1 tabs, tooltip says 'Open {displayName} ({N} tabs)'
When tabState is 'unavailable', tooltip says 'Open {displayName}'
When tabState is 'closed' and plugin has homepage, tooltip says 'Open {displayName} in new tab'
When tabState is 'closed' and plugin has no homepage, icon is not clickable (no cursor-pointer, no hover effect)
Icon has cursor-pointer when clickable and a subtle hover:scale-105 effect
Clicking the icon does NOT expand/collapse the accordion
The accordion trigger (plugin name + chevron) still works normally for expand/collapse
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx (restructure header layout) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginIcon.tsx (no changes needed — it already accepts className) Context: The current PluginCard header (PluginCard.tsx:172-231) has this structure: ```tsx
v{plugin.version}
{plugin.displayName} ...
``` The icon is currently INSIDE the AccordionPrimitive.Trigger, so clicking it expands/collapses the accordion. We need to pull it OUT of the trigger and make it an independent click target. New structure: ```tsx {/* Icon: independent click target for tab focus/open */} {tooltipText} {/* Accordion trigger: name + chevron */}
{plugin.displayName} ...
``` Key variables: ```typescript const tabCount = plugin.tabs?.length ?? 0; const hasHomepage = Boolean(plugin.homepage); const isClickable = tabCount > 0 || hasHomepage; const tooltipText = (() => { if (plugin.tabState === 'closed') { return hasHomepage ? `Open ${plugin.displayName} in new tab` : `v${plugin.version}`; } return tabCount > 1 ? `Open ${plugin.displayName} (${tabCount} tabs)` : `Open ${plugin.displayName}`; })(); const handleOpenTab = () => { void openPluginTab(plugin.name); }; ``` Constraints: - Import `openPluginTab` from `../bridge.js`. - The `

userStories.title: Update Storybook stories for PluginCard and PluginIcon with open tab behavior
userStories.description: Update PluginCard stories to demonstrate the new clickable icon behavior across all tab states (ready, unavailable, closed with homepage, closed without homepage). Add stories for multi-tab cycling tooltip. Update PluginIcon stories if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard stories include a story showing ready state with clickable icon (tooltip: 'Open Slack')
PluginCard stories include a story showing ready state with multiple tabs (tooltip: 'Open Slack (3 tabs)')
PluginCard stories include a story showing closed state with homepage (tooltip: 'Open Slack in new tab')
PluginCard stories include a story showing closed state without homepage (icon not clickable, tooltip shows version)
Mock plugin data in stories includes tabs and homepage fields where appropriate
Stories demonstrate hover:scale-105 effect on clickable icons
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.stories.tsx (update existing stories, add new ones)

Context:
The existing mockPlugin factory (PluginCard.stories.tsx) creates PluginState with:
```typescript
const mockPlugin = (overrides?: Partial<PluginState>): PluginState => ({
  name: 'slack',
  displayName: 'Slack',
  version: '0.1.0',
  permission: 'auto',
  source: 'local',
  tabState: 'ready',
  urlPatterns: ['*://*.slack.com/*'],
  sdkVersion: '0.0.3',
  reviewed: true,
  tools: [...],
  ...overrides,
});
```

Changes:
1. Update mockPlugin to include `homepage` and `tabs` where appropriate:
```typescript
const mockPlugin = (overrides?: Partial<PluginState>): PluginState => ({
  ...
  homepage: 'https://app.slack.com',
  tabs: [{ tabId: 1, url: 'https://app.slack.com/client/T123', title: 'Slack', ready: true }],
  ...overrides,
});
```

2. Add new stories:
- `ReadyMultipleTabs`: plugin with 3 tabs, demonstrating the tooltip with tab count
- `ClosedWithHomepage`: tabState='closed', homepage set, tabs undefined/empty
- `ClosedWithoutHomepage`: tabState='closed', no homepage, tabs undefined/empty — icon not clickable
- `UnavailableWithTabs`: tabState='unavailable', tabs with ready=false entries

3. Update existing `TabClosed` story to include homepage (since most plugins now have one).

4. For stories that test the icon click, the bridge function `openPluginTab` will be called but will fail silently (no background script in Storybook) — this is expected and fine. The visual behavior (tooltip, cursor, hover effect) is what we're testing in Storybook.

Constraints:
- The openPluginTab call from Storybook will throw because there's no chrome.runtime.sendMessage. This is fine — Storybook is for visual testing. If needed, add a try-catch in the click handler or mock the bridge in the story decorator.
- Follow the existing story pattern: each story uses a `*Demo` function component with `useState`.
- Use the existing ThemePair helper for at least one new story to verify dark mode appearance.

userStories.title: E2E test: open tab feature — focus existing tab and cycle through multiple tabs
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that verifies the open tab feature works end-to-end: clicking the plugin icon in the side panel focuses the matching tab, and repeated clicks cycle through multiple matching tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test opens the side panel and a test app tab, verifies clicking the plugin icon in the side panel switches focus to the test app tab
E2E test opens two test app tabs, verifies clicking the plugin icon cycles between them (first click focuses tab A, second click focuses tab B)
E2E test verifies the tooltip text shows the correct tab count
E2E test verifies the icon has cursor-pointer class/style when tabs exist
Test uses the standard E2E fixture pattern (isolated config dir, ephemeral ports)

userStories.title: E2E test: open tab feature — open homepage when no tab exists
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that verifies clicking the plugin icon when no matching tab exists opens the plugin's homepage URL in a new tab. Requires adding homepage to the e2e-test plugin.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test plugin (plugins/e2e-test) has a homepage field pointing to the test server URL
E2E test opens the side panel with no matching tabs open, clicks the plugin icon, and verifies a new tab opens at the homepage URL
After the new tab opens, the plugin transitions from 'closed' to 'unavailable' or 'ready'
The new tab's URL matches the plugin's homepage
